title: Support button can accidentally submit enclosing forms

evidence.quote:
<button
        onClick={() => setOpen(true)}
        aria-label={t("button")}
        className="fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40 flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full border border-white/10 bg-black/80 px-4 py-2.5 text-xs font-medium text-slate-300 shadow-lg hover:border-cyan-400/30 hover:text-cyan-400 transition-all duration-200"
      >

reasoning: A bare HTML button defaults to type="submit" when it is rendered inside a form. SupportButton is a reusable floating component with no guard against being mounted under a form, so clicking it can trigger form submission while also opening the support modal.
reproduction: Render SupportButton anywhere inside a form with an onSubmit handler, then click the support button. The form submit handler fires because the button has no explicit type.
recommendation: Set type="button" on the SupportButton button element.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are provided for this component, and there is no coverage showing behavior when the component is mounted inside a form.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a React component test that renders SupportButton inside a form with a submit spy, clicks the button, and asserts the submit spy was not called while the modal open action occurs.
minimumFixScope: One-line change in frontend/src/components/SupportButton.tsx to add type="button" to the button element.
